decision.title: Dual provider data plane: local SQLite plus Supabase
decision.summary: Shared app data and uploaded assets should move behind a provider contract that keeps local SQLite plus local files as the default development path while adding Supabase as the first hosted backend; do not rely on committed `.db` binaries or Git LFS for normal runtime setup.
decision.impact: Books, Listen, and future shared services can target one backend-neutral contract. Auth/session migration stays separate from the first data/storage phase.

decision.title: Supabase hosted bootstrap is standardized around one linked project plus a private reader bucket
decision.summary: The hosted bootstrap path should use one linked Supabase project ref, a private `reader-library` bucket, env-driven Payload Postgres/S3 settings in the repo, and a machine-local Codex MCP registration. Only the database password and the S3 access-key pair stay manual secrets.
decision.impact: Most of the hosted setup can now be repeated from the repo and local CLI state without committing `.db` files or keeping a vague dashboard-only checklist.

decision.title: repub-builder becomes a standalone git-installable repo and returns as a vendored submodule
decision.summary: `@portfolio/repub-builder` should move out of `packages/` and live in the standalone `MagicbornStudios/repub-builder` repository. This monorepo should consume it through a vendored submodule workspace checkout instead of carrying the package as a normal in-tree directory.
decision.impact: The package can behave like a real external library with git URL installs, while the portfolio repo keeps a pinned vendored checkout and stops coupling package structure to app-repo history.

decision.title: Published book binaries move to storage as immutable versioned artifacts while the manifest stays lightweight
decision.summary: Built EPUBs and planning-pack zips should publish into storage as immutable versioned artifacts. Payload tracks the current version, while `public/books/manifest.json` keeps only lightweight metadata plus the current remote URLs needed by the site and reader.
decision.impact: The repo stops treating shipped EPUB/zip binaries as normal commit cargo, checkpoint naming stays explicit, and SSR-facing routes can still render deterministic metadata without keeping heavyweight binaries in git.

decision.title: Public SEO surfaces may be repo-first or Postgres-backed, but storage is only for heavy binaries and private assets
decision.summary: Public pages may keep authored copy in repo or fetch it from Postgres on the server, but they must resolve through stable slug and metadata loaders. Supabase storage is for heavy binaries, media, generated bundles, originals, and private uploads rather than as the source of crawlable page copy.
decision.impact: The repo can move heavy files and mutable catalogs off git without turning public content into client-only storage reads or sacrificing server-rendered SEO surfaces.

decision.title: Public site media uses a manifest bridge during migration instead of forcing immediate content rewrites
decision.summary: Heavy public site media should publish into `site-media-assets`, while a small manifest maps legacy `/images/...` paths to storage-backed URLs. Content loaders resolve those paths at runtime so authored MDX and frontmatter can keep stable references during the migration.
decision.impact: The portfolio app can adopt storage-backed media without rewriting every existing document at once, and the same bridge can be extended to other public-media classes until a fuller DB-backed content model is worth the churn.

decision.title: Public catalogs move metadata first, while heavy files move to storage and long-form copy stays repo-first until proven otherwise
decision.summary: The next public-catalog migration should separate metadata from heavyweight files. `/apps`, `/projects`, and `/resumes` may move ordering, summaries, links, and file relationships into Postgres collections such as `site-app-records`, `project-records`, and `resume-records`, while downloadable or media-heavy files move into storage collections such as `site-download-assets`. Long-form editorial bodies can stay repo-first until moving them clearly reduces maintenance.
decision.impact: The repo can shed hard-coded arrays, local file scans, and heavy downloadable artifacts without weakening SSR or forcing all public writing into the database before that trade actually pays off.

decision.title: Public auth UI remains owner-only in v1
decision.summary: The shell/sidebar login refresh should reuse the existing seeded owner account and auth/session routes. Public visitors browse almost everything anonymously, while login only unlocks private and admin surfaces for the single owner configured by the auth env vars until a broader role model is intentionally added.
decision.impact: The UI can adopt shadcn-compatible login and account affordances without implying public signup or multi-user behavior that the backend does not support yet.

decision.title: GAD is the universal planning framework — agent-agnostic, not Claude Code-specific
decision.summary: GAD (get-anything-done) is the single planning framework for all projects in this repo. Skills are methodology documents (SKILL.md) stored in vendor/get-anything-done/skills/ — they must work with any AI coding assistant, not just Claude Code. Claude Code-specific install paths (~/.claude/) are implementation details, not the canonical source.
decision.impact: All projects register under GAD via planning-config.toml. rp-* skills are deprecated; gad:* skills are canonical. No new framework-specific skill systems are added.

decision.title: Evals capture actual content, not just byte counts — token counts are the primary efficiency metric
decision.summary: Every eval RUN.md must contain verbatim CLI and raw workflow outputs, a unit-by-unit fidelity table, token counts (chars/4), arithmetic showing all formulas applied, and an agent simulation proving the next-action decision is identical with and without the CLI. Byte counts alone are insufficient because they cannot reveal whether information is actually readable or complete.
decision.impact: All future eval runs follow the scientific format defined in vendor/get-anything-done/evals/DEFINITIONS.md. v3 is the first run that meets this standard.

decision.title: Context = session-variable information units; static docs are exempt from CLI coverage
decision.summary: Context for agent resumption is defined as the 10 session-variable information units (U1–U10) in DEFINITIONS.md. Static docs (AGENTS.md style guide, planning model reference tables, done-task history) do not constitute context loss if absent from CLI output — they are read once and cached. Context loss occurs only when a unit's absence would cause an agent to make a different next-action decision.
decision.impact: Completeness scoring is now honest: units present at reduced fidelity (Truncated/Approximated) score 0.5, not 1.0. v3 completeness = 0.85, lower than v2's inflated 0.95.

decision.title: All session intents, decisions, and guidance must be captured in planning docs each session
decision.summary: Every working session that produces new decisions, requirements, or guidance must update DECISIONS.xml (root) and the relevant section planning docs before the session ends. Chat context is ephemeral — planning docs are the durable record. The GAD planning loop is the mechanism; this decision makes the capture requirement explicit.
decision.impact: Agents starting a new session get current decisions from the planning docs, not from reconstructing chat history. Drift between chat intent and planning state is prevented.

decision.title: repo-planner is officially a GAD project; rp-* skills are deprecated
decision.summary: vendor/repo-planner may remain registered as a GAD root (id=repo-planner) in planning-config.toml for CLI wiring. The submodule tracks upstream RepoPlanner main at the pre-skills framework pin (no rp-* directories inside RepoPlanner; skills experiment preserved on upstream branch development only). Monorepo skills/rp-* are thin stubs to vendor/get-anything-done/skills/. Manuscript and other methodology live under get-anything-done. Portfolio repo-planner docs sink may need repointing after the submodule pin (see REPOPLANNER-TO-GAD-MIGRATION-GAPS.md).
decision.impact: gad snapshot can still list repo-planner if configured; primary agent methodology is GAD skills. No new rp-* bodies in this monorepo beyond stubs.

decision.title: Phase-one owner account UI reads avatar data from the shared user record and session payload
decision.summary: The first owner-shell pass should not stop at initials-only identity. The shared user model and `/api/auth/session` payload should carry an avatar URL so the sidebar can render a SaaS-style account footer immediately, while still falling back to initials when no avatar exists.
decision.impact: The owner shell now exposes a real account card without coupling the UI to Supabase-specific helpers, and future org-user work can reuse the same session contract.

decision.title: The first mounted Payload admin pass stays focused on owner-managed collections and hides internal maintenance tables
decision.summary: The upstream `(payload)` route group should own `/admin`, but the first customization pass should only foreground collections that behave like operational content surfaces. Internal reader-state and RAG maintenance collections stay hidden until their workflows are intentionally designed, while public-read asset collections keep public read access and gain owner write access through Payload.
decision.impact: The mounted admin feels like a usable owner back office immediately instead of exposing every internal record table, and the existing runtime/public asset flows keep working while Payload becomes the editing surface for the collections that belong there.

decision.title: Cross-project observability stays lightweight: public Vercel Analytics plus request-id tracing on sensitive server routes
decision.summary: The portfolio and Grime Time public layouts should use Vercel Analytics for cheap pageview insight, but analytics events must strip query strings and fragments, ignore internal or admin paths, and avoid emails or other PII. Server-side observability for this pass stays small: generate request ids, return them in response headers, and log safe route summaries plus timings for high-signal Grime Time routes instead of dumping raw request payloads.
decision.impact: The repo gets usable traffic and route-level debugging signals quickly without committing to a full observability platform or leaking sensitive form and copilot data into analytics or ad hoc logs.

decision.title: GAD CLI is format-agnostic: XML, MD, MDX all valid in .planning/
decision.summary: Sub-repos and .planning/ directories may use XML, MD, or MDX. The GAD CLI must parse all three transparently. The planning sink normalizes to MDX only. compile() converts any format to MDX; decompile() restores the original format. Coding agents and the CLI treat methodology as the constant — format is an implementation detail.
decision.impact: gad sink validate checks parseability and round-trip fidelity, not format compliance. grim-time-site can stay XML. Any new project may choose its format.

decision.title: gad-manuscript is a self-contained domain translator repo
decision.summary: gad-manuscript is not registered in the portfolio planning-config.toml. It is a standalone repo that defines the domain translator pattern — adapts GAD base methodology to book/fiction creation. It owns its own SKILL.md files, evals, and planning docs while referencing gad:* base skills.
decision.impact: Domain translator pattern is now defined. Other verticals (product, design) follow the same pattern: standalone repo, own skills, own evals, references gad:* base.

decision.title: GAD v1.0.0 defining characteristic: skills-first with evaluation
decision.summary: Pre-1.0 (GSD/ralph-wiggum loop) was workflow-heavy — agents followed long workflow docs. v1.0.0 is skills-first: every skill has an agent-agnostic SKILL.md definition, is evaluated for output quality, and is versioned with the methodology. The CLI provides context efficiency. Evals are scientific. Traces measure what agents retrieve. Domain translators extend GAD to verticals.
decision.impact: All new skills must have SKILL.md in vendor/get-anything-done/skills/. Skills are evaluated via the eval framework. Methodology version is tracked in package.json gadMethodologyVersion field.

decision.title: gad context inlines file contents by default; --refs is the lightweight mode
decision.summary: The `gad context` command now inlines all planning file contents directly into its output by default. This gives agents a single command to get full context without reading files separately. The `--refs` flag restores the old lightweight behavior (file paths only). This improves the completeness score on the cli-efficiency eval — agents no longer need to issue secondary read commands after `gad context`.
decision.impact: Agents must use `gad context --refs` when they only want file paths. `gad context` without flags is now verbose — suitable for session start, not for routine orientation checks.

decision.title: gad snapshot is the one-shot full project context command
decision.summary: `gad snapshot` inlines every planning file in a project's .planning/ directory (excluding archive/, sessions/, node_modules/). Priority order: STATE, ROADMAP, REQUIREMENTS, DECISIONS, TASK-REGISTRY, then alphabetical. This is the canonical command for handing an agent a complete cold-start context pack. It differs from `gad context` in scope — context gives session-aware minimal refs, snapshot gives everything.
decision.impact: Agents starting a fresh session on an unfamiliar project use `gad snapshot --project `. `gad context` is for ongoing work within a session.

decision.title: gad sink is the canonical docs sync command; gad docs compile is an alias
decision.summary: `gad sink` owns the four-direction docs sync surface: status (diff), compile (planning→docs), decompile (docs→planning), validate (mapping health). `gad docs compile` remains for backward compatibility but is an alias path through the same docs-compiler.cjs library. The sink is manual by default (sink_workflow = "manual") — no auto-compile on file change.
decision.impact: New code uses `gad sink compile` not `gad docs compile`. The docs-compiler.cjs library is shared. `gad sink decompile` is the canonical way to pull docs edits back into planning files.

decision.title: Missing-arg UX: print available choices and rerun hint, never a blind error
decision.summary: All GAD CLI commands that require a `--project` or `--id` argument must, when that argument is absent, print the list of valid choices and a ready-to-run rerun hint on stderr before exiting 1. This applies to: eval run/score/diff (list eval projects), session resume/close (list active sessions). Blind citty "Missing required argument" errors are banned — required: true is replaced with our own check.
decision.impact: Any future command added to gad.cjs that takes a required project/session/id arg must follow this pattern. The listEvalProjectsHint() and listActiveSessionsHint() helpers are the canonical implementation.

decision.title: TRACE.json is the machine-readable sidecar for every eval run
decision.summary: Every eval run directory must contain a TRACE.json alongside RUN.md and SCORE.md. TRACE.json records: workflow (A/B/both), per-command token counts and unit fidelity, unit_coverage map (U1–U12 at Full/Referenced/Truncated/Approximated/Absent), and computed totals (tokens, completeness). Agents write it manually using `gad eval trace write` or inline during a run. The trace is the input to `gad eval trace diff/report` for trend analysis across runs.
decision.impact: Any new eval run must produce a TRACE.json. Backfill is done via `gad eval trace write` using data from the RUN.md. The diff command surfaces regressions and improvements per information unit. The report command shows persistent problem units across the full run history of a project.

decision.title: gad state and gad phases default to session project when a session is active
decision.summary: When no --projectid flag is given, `gad state` and `gad phases` call `getActiveSessionProjectId()` which reads the most-recent non-closed session JSON and returns its projectId. If found, output is scoped to that project only. If the projectId doesn't match any registered root (shouldn't happen), falls back to all projects. Explicit `--projectid` overrides the session default. This drops CLI tokens from 2,341 (v5, 3 projects) to 1,104 (v6, 1 project) while keeping completeness = 1.000. token_reduction: 0.874 → 0.940. Composite: 0.950 → 0.976.
decision.impact: Agents working in a session see only their project state by default — no extra flag. Multi-project output still available via explicit --projectid or when no session exists. All three eval targets hit simultaneously for the first time in v6.

decision.title: portfolio-bare eval uses real agent sessions, not a harness — context_mode is self-declared
decision.summary: The portfolio-bare eval is not an automated harness. It is a real agent session in a template directory (evals/portfolio-bare/template/) that contains only planning docs and requirements — no app code. The agent opens the template in Claude Code or Codex, runs `gad session new [--fresh]` to declare context mode, plans phases from requirements, executes tasks, and writes a trace at the end. context_mode (fresh|loaded) is stored in the session JSON and stamped on each task in TASK-REGISTRY.xml. context_delta emerges from comparing runs across context modes over time. The user is responsible for declaring the correct context mode. Skills: `gad:eval-run` (running the eval), `gad:eval-report` (comparing runs).
decision.impact: No API keys, no execSync harness. The eval IS real agent work. The report (`gad eval trace report --project portfolio-bare`) shows which tasks are context-dependent vs requirements-derivable. A task that only appears in loaded runs is one the agent misses when starting cold — that is the most actionable finding.

decision.title: RepoPlanner is deprecated — GAD is the sole planning product
decision.summary: The MagicbornStudios/RepoPlanner repository is frozen/archived; no new features. This monorepo migrates to GAD (`vendor/get-anything-done`) for CLI, workflows, and (via phase 06) official React UI embedded in the portfolio. Remaining `loop-cli` calls use `scripts/run-legacy-repo-planner-cli.mjs` with deprecation until init/embed/report paths are ported. **Exception (narrow):** upstream `apps/landing` (Vercel reference site) may receive **build-only** fixes: direct npm deps for transitive peers the embed pulls in, plus `postinstall`/`prebuild` script `link-landing-deps-to-repo-root.mjs` that symlinks landing `node_modules` into repo-root `node_modules` so TypeScript (resolving from `components/**`) finds `react`, `next`, `@types/*`, etc. when npm hoists to `apps/landing` only — no product features or roadmap work.
decision.impact: Agents and docs must prefer `gad` and `.planning` + `gad-config.toml`. Portfolio eventually drops the `repo-planner` package dependency entirely. Track landing build hygiene under phase 06 task **06-04** and [repo-planner planning docs](/docs/repo-planner/planning/planning-docs).
